nuggets.headline: radiohead's thom yorke once played glastonbury unable to hear himself perform
nuggets.text: Imagine headlining one of the world's biggest festivals, the Pyramid Stage at Glastonbury in 1997, only to have the sound system fail during your set. That's exactly what happened to Thom Yorke and Radiohead amidst a massive downpour, meaning they couldn't hear what they were playing. Despite the technical disaster, many still consider it one of their greatest gigs.

source.title: 25 things Radiohead have done since OK Computer
source.quoteSnippet: Radiohead's front-man wasn't entirely keen to perform at Glastonbury back in 1997. In June 1997, just a few weeks after OK Computer came out, Radiohead headlinedGlastonbury's Pyramid Stage for the first time... The rain fell and the sound system failed during Radiohead's set, so the band couldn't actually hear what they were doing.

nuggets.imageCaption: Thom Yorke on stage in 2017, years after his challenging 1997 Glastonbury performance.

nuggets.headline: the guitar arpeggios in "weird fishes/arpeggi" are distinct, layered parts
nuggets.text: The song's title, "Arpeggi," is a direct nod to its core musical structure. What you're hearing are not just complex chords, but three distinct guitar arpeggios, played by Ed O'Brien, Thom Yorke, and Jonny Greenwood, layered together. Each part has its own subtly shifted accents, creating a rich, shimmering texture that builds the song's momentum.

source.title: Harmonic Analysis of Radiohead’s “Weird Fishes/Arpeggi”
source.quoteSnippet: The first guitar arpeggio (the plural of which gives the song its title) is played by Ed O’Brien... On the fourth chord in the progression... a second guitar (in live versions, this is played by Thom Yorke) superimposes another arpeggio... A third arpeggio, played by Jonny Greenwood, is added to the texture at 1:48... this layering of different accents becomes more evident... it forms a shimmeringeffect

nuggets.imageCaption: A musical transcription of the layered guitar arpeggios in Radiohead's song.

nuggets.headline: radiohead's thom yorke has collaborated with the electronic artist burial
nuggets.text: If you're drawn to the atmospheric depth of Radiohead, you absolutely need to hear Burial. Thom Yorke himself has teamed up with the enigmatic electronic producer Burial and Four Tet on multiple 12" vinyl releases, creating experimental electronic tracks that share a similar sense of intricate texture and mood.

source.title: Similar artists - Thom Yorke | Last.fm
source.quoteSnippet: In March 2011,Burial,Four Tet, andThom YorkeofRadioheadteamed up to release a 12" vinyl containing "Ego" and "Mirror"... In late 2020 the three of them released a single on XL Recordings, 'Her Revolution' b/w 'His Rope'.

nuggets.imageCaption: Burial, known for his atmospheric electronic music and collaborations with Thom Yorke.

artistSummary: Radiohead, the English alternative rock band from Abingdon, Oxfordshire, formed in the mid-1980s when the members were still in school. Consisting of Thom Yorke, Jonny Greenwood, Colin Greenwood, Ed O’Brien, and Phil Selway, they've built a prolific and acclaimed catalog, evolving from college rock to electronics-embracing art rock. They've consistently worked with producer Nigel Godrich and artist Stanley Donwood since 1994.
